evidence: cargo test --all-targets failed in cli::tests::risk::run_risk_rule_set_industry_blocklist_dispatches_to_handler under default parallel test execution; the same test passed when run alone or with --test-threads=1
rule: Phase 1 cargo test gate; risk CLI tests must be isolated and deterministic
impact: The failing assertion was a test isolation bug, not a production persistence defect: src/cli/tests/risk.rs mutated global QUANTIX_RISK_PATH/QUANTIX_TRADE_PATH while other test modules in the same binary also mutated process env, so the risk CLI test could read the wrong path under parallel execution and block the gate
reproduction: cargo test --all-targets; cargo test --lib -- --test-threads=1; cargo test --lib cli::tests::risk::run_risk_rule_set_industry_blocklist_dispatches_to_handler
recommended_fix: Serialize env-mutating tests behind a shared process-wide lock or move these tests to a non-parallel harness; preserve and restore QUANTIX_RISK_PATH/QUANTIX_TRADE_PATH around the test body
acceptance_criteria: The risk CLI test passes both alone and in the default suite, and crate-local env-mutating unit tests now share a process-wide lock
tests_required: cargo test --lib; cargo test --lib -- --test-threads=1; cargo test --lib cli::tests::risk::run_risk_rule_set_industry_blocklist_dispatches_to_handler

evidence: After AUDIT-S1-001 was fixed, cargo test --all-targets failed in catalog_lists_and_computes_ts_rank_close_5 with expected null_count=12 but actual null_count=17
rule: Phase 1 cargo test gate; factor pipeline tests must encode missing-value semantics correctly
impact: The test fixture contains three symbols over ten days and one explicit missing close for 600000.SH on day 5. A five-day rolling rank with KeepNull produces 12 warmup nulls plus five null windows affected by that missing close, so 17 is the correct null count
reproduction: cargo test --all-targets; cargo test --test factor_pipeline_test catalog_lists_and_computes_ts_rank_close_5 -- --nocapture
recommended_fix: Correct the assertion to reflect rolling-window null propagation and document the expected count
acceptance_criteria: The factor pipeline test passes alone and cargo test --all-targets passes
tests_required: cargo test --test factor_pipeline_test; cargo test --all-targets

evidence: init_polars mutates the process environment with unsafe std::env::set_var(POLARS_MAX_THREADS, ...) and is called from async CLI run_init after the Tokio runtime is active
rule: Phase 2 unsafe review; Rust 2024 marks process environment mutation unsafe because concurrent environment access can be unsound
impact: Global environment mutation during runtime initialization can race with other threads or libraries reading environment variables, and the unsafe block has no documented safety precondition
reproduction: cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features; manual trace: GitNexus init_polars callers include src/cli/handlers/app_shell.rs:99
recommended_fix: Move Polars thread configuration to process startup before async runtime/thread creation, use a Polars-supported configuration API if available, or document and enforce a single-threaded pre-runtime safety boundary
acceptance_criteria: No production unsafe env mutation occurs from async handlers, or the unsafe block has an enforced and reviewed safety invariant; related tests cover initialization behavior
tests_required: cargo test --lib analysis::polars_adapter::tests::test_init_polars; cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features

evidence: BatchProcessor::process_in_batches now requires each callback to return Result<()> and propagates the first batch error
rule: Phase 2 discarded-result review; public batch API should not silently drop callback failure information
impact: A caller that passes a fallible callback now receives Err immediately instead of Ok(progress) when a batch fails; current GitNexus callers are bench/test only, so observed production blast radius is low
reproduction: Manual review; GitNexus process_in_batches incoming calls are bench_batch_processing and test_batch_processor
recommended_fix: Constrain process_fn to return Result or an explicit per-batch outcome and propagate/update errors, or change the API name/signature to make fire-and-forget semantics explicit
acceptance_criteria: A failing batch callback is reflected in the returned Result or BatchProgress error count, and tests cover callback failure behavior
tests_required: cargo test --lib io::batch::tests::test_batch_processor; cargo test --lib io::batch::tests::process_in_batches_returns_callback_error; cargo test --lib io::batch
status:
fixed
AUDIT-S3-002,S3,confirmed,tasks/scheduler,src/tasks/scheduler.rs:90,TaskScheduler::add_task now propagates scheduler.remove(job_id).await failure when replacing an existing scheduled task before scheduling the new task"
column14: Phase 2 discarded-result review; scheduler mutation failures should not be silently ignored
column15: If old job removal fails, add_task now returns Err before replacing the in-memory task or scheduling a new job; current GitNexus callers are tests only but the API is public scheduler infrastructure
column16: Manual review; GitNexus add_task incoming calls currently only include scheduler tests; regression test uses a test-only failure injection because tokio-cron-scheduler remove returns Ok for unknown job IDs
column17: Propagate remove failure and abort replacement before scheduling the new job
column18: Replacing a running task cannot silently leave the old job active; failure is surfaced to the caller and the previous task entry is preserved
column19: cargo test --lib tasks::scheduler::tests::add_task_returns_error_when_replacement_job_removal_fails; cargo test --lib tasks::scheduler; cargo fmt --check

evidence: WebSocketClient message loop now handles tx.send(quote) failure when the realtime quote receiver has been dropped
rule: Phase 2 discarded-result review; streaming data delivery failures should be observable
impact: When the consumer side is gone, quote dispatch now clears the stale handler, returns an error, logs the disconnected receiver, and exits the current WebSocket stream loop instead of silently dropping quotes
reproduction: Manual review of WebSocketClient::connect message loop; GitNexus impact for connect and WebSocketClient is LOW with no indexed upstream processes
recommended_fix: Handle send failure by logging and clearing the handler, breaking/reconnecting, or transitioning to a disconnected/error state according to the intended lifecycle
acceptance_criteria: Dropped receiver is observable and the stale sender is cleared; targeted async test covers closed receiver behavior
tests_required: cargo test --lib sources::websocket::tests::dispatch_quote_returns_error_when_receiver_is_dropped; cargo test --lib sources::websocket; cargo fmt --check

evidence: TradingCalendar uses host-local time for A-share session decisions and unwraps local datetime construction
rule: Phase 2 boundary unwrap review
impact: Host timezone can shift trading-session classification; local datetime unwrap can panic on timezone gaps or ambiguity
reproduction: Manual review; caller search shows collect scheduler uses get_current_status at src/tasks/collect_scheduler.rs:153
recommended_fix: Use an explicit China market timezone and avoid unwrap on local datetime construction
acceptance_criteria: Trading status is host-timezone independent and production calendar paths do not unwrap local datetime construction
tests_required: Add trading-calendar timezone/session tests; cargo test --lib core::trading_calendar

evidence: BatchProcessor::new now rejects zero batch_size and zero max_concurrent_tasks with typed QuantixError values
rule: Phase 2 public API boundary review
impact: batch_size=0 can no longer panic in div_ceil/chunks paths through a constructed BatchProcessor; max_concurrent_tasks=0 can no longer create a zero-permit batch_import semaphore through the public constructor
reproduction: Manual review; GitNexus reports no current incoming calls for batch_import, so observed blast radius is low but API risk remains; regression tests cover both invalid config fields
recommended_fix: Validate BatchConfig in BatchProcessor::new or expose a fallible constructor that rejects zero batch size and zero concurrency
acceptance_criteria: Invalid batch config is rejected with a typed error and tests cover zero batch_size and zero max_concurrent_tasks
tests_required: cargo test --lib io::batch; cargo test --benches --no-run
